summary: PR 2361 linked src/melee/ft/ftwaitanim.c by marking it Matching in configure.py, matching ftCo_8008A7A8 according to the bot report, and cleaning related fighter wait-animation types and symbol metadata. The core code change manually inlined ftCo_8008A6D8 inside ftCo_8008A7A8 with an explicit TODO saying it was the only way the author could make it match.

key_files.role: Primary matched source file; ftCo_8008A7A8 was restructured and manually inlined ftCo_8008A6D8 logic was added.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: src/melee/ft/ftwaitanim.c hunk replacing OSReport/__assert with HSD_ASSERTREPORT and expanding ftCo_8008A7A8
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot reports main/melee/ft/ftwaitanim ftCo_8008A7A8 moved from 99.86% to 100.00%

key_files.role: Build configuration changed ftwaitanim.c from NonMatching to Matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: configure.py Object(NonMatching, "melee/ft/ftwaitanim.c") changed to Object(Matching, "melee/ft/ftwaitanim.c")

key_files.role: Renamed and refined wait-animation data types used by fighter data and ftwaitanim.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: struct S_TEMP4 renamed to struct Fighter_WaitAnimData
raw_diff: Fighter_WaitAnimData::xC changed from ftSubactionList* to union CmdUnion*
raw_diff: ftSubactionList struct removed

key_files.role: Updated uses and casts from S_TEMP4 to Fighter_WaitAnimData.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftData_80085B98 and ftData_80085CD8 locals/casts updated to struct Fighter_WaitAnimData*

key_files.role: Updated Fighter_ChangeMotionState local type to Fighter_WaitAnimData.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: unk_struct_x18 type changed from struct S_TEMP4* to struct Fighter_WaitAnimData*
raw_diff: typedef struct ftSubactionList ftSubactionList removed

key_files.role: Adjusted symbol metadata for local labels, local string/float objects, and bss/data labels affected by the link.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: efAlt_* local symbols replaced with anonymous @70x labels in .data, .sdata, and .sdata2
raw_diff: local scope added to several @20x/@25x/@26x Ground-related strings
raw_diff: ...bss.0 label added at efLib_AnimQueue address

what_changed: Marked melee/ft/ftwaitanim.c as Matching in configure.py.
Matched ftCo_8008A7A8 according to the bot report, increasing that function from 99.86% to 100.00%.
Replaced an OSReport plus raw __assert sequence in getAnimID with HSD_ASSERTREPORT(86, 0, "wait anim data illegal!!\n", max).
Renamed struct S_TEMP4 to struct Fighter_WaitAnimData and propagated the type through Fighter, ftData, ftwaitanim, and Fighter_ChangeMotionState uses.
Changed Fighter_WaitAnimData::xC from ftSubactionList* to union CmdUnion*, allowing assignment to fp->x3E4_fighterCmdScript.u without a cast.
Removed the now-unused ftSubactionList forward declaration and struct definition.
Adjusted GALE01 symbol metadata by converting several efAlt_* names to compiler-style local @ labels, adding local scope annotations, and adding local labels such as ...data.0 and ...bss.0.

smart_moves.text: Used a real assert/report macro instead of preserving expanded OSReport plus __assert code.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: getAnimID changed OSReport + __assert to HSD_ASSERTREPORT

smart_moves.text: Recovered a more meaningful wait-animation struct name and a more accurate command-script pointer type, eliminating a cast in ftCo_8008A6D8.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: S_TEMP4 renamed to Fighter_WaitAnimData
raw_diff: xC changed to union CmdUnion*
raw_diff: cast removed from fp->x3E4_fighterCmdScript.u assignment

smart_moves.text: Kept the manual inlining workaround visibly documented with a TODO instead of hiding it as ordinary source style.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_8008A7A8 TODO says manually inlined ftCo_8008A6D8, not clean, only way author could make it match

decomp_lessons.lesson: When an expanded report-plus-assert sequence has a matching project macro form with the same line and message behavior, HSD_ASSERTREPORT can represent the authored source more cleanly.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: OSReport("wait anim data illegal!!\n", max) and __assert("ftwaitanim.c", 86, "0") replaced by HSD_ASSERTREPORT(86, 0, "wait anim data illegal!!\n", max)

decomp_lessons.lesson: A matched function may require inlining a helper at the callsite; if retained, the workaround should be narrow and explicitly documented because it is not clean source style.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_8008A7A8 manually inlines ftCo_8008A6D8 body
raw_diff: TODO documents manual inline as not clean but needed to match
text_corpus: bot reports ftCo_8008A7A8 reached 100.00%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Once a temporary struct's role and field type become clearer, propagate the typed name through related headers and users to reduce casts and obsolete placeholder declarations.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: S_TEMP4 renamed to Fighter_WaitAnimData across multiple files
raw_diff: xC typed as union CmdUnion* and cast removed in ftwaitanim.c
raw_diff: ftSubactionList typedef and struct removed

naming_conventions.name: Fighter_WaitAnimData
naming_conventions.convention: Use a semantic subsystem/owner-prefixed struct name when the data role is supported by local use; here x24 wait-animation data replaces placeholder S_TEMP4.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: struct S_TEMP4 renamed to struct Fighter_WaitAnimData
raw_diff: Fighter::x24 changed to struct Fighter_WaitAnimData*
raw_diff: ftwaitanim.c indexes fp->x24 as wait animation data

naming_conventions.name: union CmdUnion* xC
naming_conventions.convention: Prefer the concrete command-script union type over an ad-hoc ftSubactionList placeholder when assigning into fighterCmdScript.u.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Fighter_WaitAnimData::xC changed from ftSubactionList* to union CmdUnion*
raw_diff: fp->x3E4_fighterCmdScript.u = anim->xC no longer needs a cast

assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: ftCo_8008A7A8 keeps the normal helper call for the early-return case but manually expands ftCo_8008A6D8 in the alternate path. The source includes a TODO that this was not clean but was the only way the author could make it match.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_8008A7A8 adds scoped block containing blend_data, anim, GET_FIGHTER, ftData_80085CD8, ftCo_8009E7B4, ftAnim_8006EBE8, and ftAnim_8006EBA4
raw_diff: TODO comment documents manual inline
text_corpus: bot reports ftCo_8008A7A8 reached 100.00%

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Control-flow reshaping with early return and scoped locals
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: The function introduces a local temp for fp->anim_id, returns after the simple helper-call path, then declares temp and anim_id in a nested block for the random wait-animation path.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_8008A7A8 adds enum_t temp, early return, and nested enum_t temp, anim_id block

assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: The PR changes several local data labels and scope annotations in symbols.txt, including replacing named efAlt_* locals with anonymous @ labels and adding local labels.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: config/GALE01/symbols.txt efAlt_* to @70x changes
raw_diff: ...data.0 and ...bss.0 labels added

source_update_candidates.title: Manual inlining should remain explicitly justified and scoped
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2361 is a concrete example of manual helper inlining retained for a match with an inline TODO stating it is not clean. Curator may use it as an example under the existing matching-tactics evidence standard rather than as broad approval for manual inlining.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_8008A7A8 TODO and inline block
text_corpus: bot reports matched function
source_update_candidates.reason: Candidate example for an existing standard; evidence does not support promoting manual inlining as normal style.

rejection_notes: Do not infer from this PR that manual inlining is generally preferred; the diff itself calls it not clean and only justified for this match.
Do not treat the symbols.txt efAlt-to-@label changes as a global naming convention without additional symbol ownership evidence.
Do not claim human reviewer approval or requested changes; the loaded slice has no human reviews or review comments.
Do not claim broad validation beyond the bot report; no local build command, objdiff transcript, or adjacent regression check is included.
